channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump to impose double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries
item.description: The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.
item.content:encoded:
The United States will slap taxes of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries accounting for 99% of U.S. imports, charging that they have inadequately enforced bans on goods produced by forced labor.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How to keep an ancient text interesting to students? Make it a summer blockbuster
item.description: English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.
item.content:encoded:
English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.

(Image credit: Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal Pic/Melinda Sue Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Egyptian warrior princesses, ancient avocados, and more science news
item.description: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.
item.content:encoded: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Trump cuts target federal advisory boards. What does it mean for agencies like NASA?
item.description: The Trump administration has terminated or suspended more than 100 federal advisory committees. Former members say agencies like NASA now have less accountability to stakeholders and the public.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has terminated or suspended more than 100 federal advisory committees. Former members say agencies like NASA now have less accountability to stakeholders and the public.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Invasive species cause more harm in the Global South
item.description: Invasive species can wreak havoc on ecosystems. A new study in Science suggests low- and middle-income countries in the Global South experience greater harms.
item.content:encoded: Invasive species can wreak havoc on ecosystems. A new study in Science suggests low- and middle-income countries in the Global South experience greater harms.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Trump allies are poised to weaken protections for historic sites — not just in D.C.
item.description: A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.
item.content:encoded:
A council packed with Trump appointees is poised to rewrite historic preservation rules. Critics say that could help him build his D.C. arch faster — and endanger important sites across the country.

(Image credit: Jen Golbeck)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump
item.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.
item.content:encoded:
Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
item.description: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.content:encoded: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
item.dc:creator: Jan Camenzind Broomby

item.title: Republicans urge Justice Department to investigate former prosecutor Jack Smith
item.description: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.
item.content:encoded:
The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
item.description: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.
item.content:encoded: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman
